title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots plan to pick up Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option worth $18.1 million, extending his contract through 2027.
Coach Mike Vrabel expressed desire for a long-term extension to retain Gonzalez beyond his current contract.
Gonzalez, a 2023 first-round pick, earned a Pro Bowl selection and showed strong playoff performance despite past injuries.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has scored five goals and two assists in his last six games for the Bruins.
The Bruins have achieved their fourth consecutive victory, defeating the Dallas Stars 6-3.
David Pastrnak leads with a career-best 66 assists this season, contributing 23 points in the last 14 games.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson of Everett is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson runs to honor trauma teams and recalls the impact of the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings.
She views participating in the marathon as a personal journey, connecting her past experiences with Boston's resilience.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit due to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
Boston's $48.4 million deficit represents about 1% of the city's $4.8 billion operating budget, with $1.2 billion in reserves.
Councilors criticize the Wu administration's handling of the deficit, calling for transparency and suggesting audits and spending reductions.

title: Squirrels, spinach doughnuts, and sirens: New England’s best April Fools’ jokes
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New England celebrated April Fools’ Day 2026 with unique pranks involving squirrels, spinach doughnuts, and unexpected sirens.
Local businesses and residents devised creative jokes that captured the playful spirit of the annual April 1st tradition.
The pranks covered various themes, showcasing the region's diverse sense of humor and communal participation in April Fools’ activities.

title: Where do the ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ live?
author: Cameron Sperance
bullets: Elizabeth McGraw and Alicia Carmody feature prominently in 'Real Housewives of Rhode Island,' filmed in various Rhode Island locations.
Cranston, known for its mix of architecture, serves as a central filming location with homes ranging from $300,000 to $1.5 million.
Newport, East Greenwich, and Providence's East Side are highlighted as aspirational areas with luxury real estate values exceeding $900,000.

title: She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Leslie Wallus is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital.
Her daughter, Eloise, survived a brain tumor thanks to care at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Wallus aims to support Eloise's pediatric oncology unit through her marathon participation.

title: She’s running Boston to support people with visual impairments
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas, 46, from Jakarta, Indonesia, is participating in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Miraningtyas runs to support the Massachusetts Association for the Blind and Visually Impaired.
She is motivated by personal experiences with eye surgery to help others afford similar treatments.

title: Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mohamed Yahiya, 47, was arrested for a scheme involving fraudulent SNAP transactions at Adham’s Smoke Shop in Chicopee.
Yahiya allegedly directed purchases of bulk Red Bull with SNAP benefits and bought them back for cash at a discount.
Police found unstamped cigarettes and marijuana at the smoke shop, leading to multiple charges including food stamp trafficking.

title: Free speech rally planned outside BU president’s office amid Pride flag dispute
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rally outside BU President Melissa Gilliam's office scheduled Thursday over Pride flag removal due to signage policy.
Terrier Courage, with over 1,800 signatures, demands policy revision allowing visible signage on doors and windows.
Petition claims BU's signage policy undermines free speech and hinders open expression on campus.
